ORDER TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT , ORDER JUDGMENT OF NOV-16-2015 SET ASIDE
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-23-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALEXEI KUCHINSKY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF COUNSEL DID NOT NOTIFY THE COURT OR THE OPPOSING SIDE OF ITS INTENT TO CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, AND/OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 473. DEFENDANT REASONABLY BELIEVED THAT THE MATTER WOULD BE ADJUDICATED BY BINDING ARBITRATION AND A TRIAL WOULD NOT OCCUR GIVEN THAT THE COURT ORDERED THE PARTIES TO ARBITRATE. THROUGH AN OVERSIGHT, DEFENDANT DID NOT ASK FOR AND THE COURT DID NOT ISSUE A STAY ORDER. THE COURT VACATES THE JUDGMENT AND ORDERS THIS ACTION STAYED. PLAINTIFF SHALL INITIATE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS BECAUSE IT IS THE PARTY WITH THE CLAIM. THERE IS NO RES JUDICATA ISSUE BECAUSE THERE IS ONLY ONE JUDGMENT. THE ISSUE OF WAIVER SHOULD HAVE BEEN RAISED IN RESPONSE TO THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. COURT SIGNS DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-03-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALEXEI KUCHINSKY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S COMMON COUNT CAUSES OF ACTION ARE WELL-PLEADED. THE ONLY ESSENTIAL ALLEGATIONS ARE (1) THE STATEMENT OF INDEBTEDNESS IN A CERTAIN SUM, (2) THE CONSIDERATION, AND (3) NONPAYMENT. SEE WITKIN, CAL. PROCEDURE, PLEADING 518. DEFENDANT'S CITATION TO OTWORTH V. SO. PACIFIC TRANS. CO. (1985) 166 CAL.APP.3D 452, IS OFF POINT BECAUSE PLAINTIFF IS NOT SUING FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL DEMURRERS ARE IMPROPER BECAUSE THIS IS A LIMITED JURISDICTION CASE. SEE CCP 92(C). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-23-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. STEPHEN ZELLER, ESQ. of Winn Law Group, Tel No. 714-446-6686 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court notes that Plaintiff counsel did not notify the Court or the opposing side of its intent to contest the tentative ruling. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALEXEI KUCHINSKY's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment is granted. Defendant demonstrates mistake, inadvertence, surprise, and/or excusable neglect within the meaning of CCP 473. Defendant reasonably believed that the matter would be adjudicated by binding arbitration and a trial would not occur given that the court ordered the parties to arbitrate. Through an oversight, defendant did not ask for and the court did not issue a stay order. The court vacates the judgment and orders this action stayed. Plaintiff shall initiate arbitration proceedings because it is the party with the claim. There is no res judicata issue because there is only one judgment. The issue of waiver should have been raised in response to the motion to compel arbitration. COURT SIGNS DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2015 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-16-2015 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Department 206 for Short Cause Trial/Prove Up. Department 514, Judge: Charles Crompton, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6840.

Brandon Tang, Esq., appearing specially for Plaintiff Discover Bank.
No appearance by or on behalf of Defendant Alexei Kuchinsky.
Declaration of Live Witness pursuant to CCP 98, submitted in lieu of testimony.
Court ordered Defendants Does 1-10 dismissed without prejudice.

After evidence, Court ordered the following Judgment entered in favor of Plaintiff CDiscover Bank and against Defendant Alexei Kuchinsky as follows:
Principal $8518.92
Pre-Judgment Interest $0.00
Attorney's Fees $0.00
Costs $501.25
Total Judgment in the amount of $8020.17

Judgment signed in open court.

Notice Of Defendant Alexei Kuchinskys Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-19-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALEXEI KUCHINSKY'S Motion To Compel Arbitration is granted. No opposition filed. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-26-2015 Set For Trial On Nov-16-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Casey Jensen, of Winn Law Group, Tel. 714.446.6686 ext 203, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ALEXEI KUCHINSKY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiff's common count causes of action are well-pleaded. The only essential allegations are (1) the statement of indebtedness in a certain sum, (2) the consideration, and (3) nonpayment. See Witkin, Cal. Procedure, Pleading 518. Defendant's citation to Otworth v. So. Pacific Trans. Co. (1985) 166 Cal.App.3d 452, is off point because plaintiff is not suing for breach of contract. Defendant's special demurrers are improper because this is a limited jurisdiction case. See CCP 92(c). Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
